---
title: "vizathon"
author: "Raj Barai"
date: "2025-11-04"
---

```{r}
library(readr)
StormEvents <- read_csv("StormEvents.csv")
View(StormEvents)
```

```{r}
head(StormEvents)
```

```{r}
str(StormEvents)
```

```{r}
colnames(StormEvents)
```

```{r}
dim(StormEvents)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
# Use the StormEvents data object
data <- StormEvents
```

```{r}
print("Available objects in your environment:")
print(ls())
```

```{r}
# 1. Count total NAs per column
na_per_column <- data %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "column", values_to = "na_count") %>%
  mutate(na_percentage = round(na_count / nrow(data) * 100, 2)) %>%
  arrange(desc(na_count))

print(na_per_column)
```

```{r}
# Create a cleaned dataset based on your analysis needs
# For example, focusing on events with location data:
storms_with_location <- StormEvents %>%
  filter(!is.na(BEGIN_LAT) & !is.na(BEGIN_LON))

# Or create a dataset for specific event types:
tornadoes <- StormEvents %>%
  filter(str_detect(EVENT_TYPE, "Tornado"))
```

```{r}
# Exploratory Data Analysis for Storm Events
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(gridExtra)
```

```{r}
# 1) TOP EVENT TYPES ANALYSIS
# Most common event types
top_events <- StormEvents %>%
  count(EVENT_TYPE, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15) %>%
  mutate(EVENT_TYPE = fct_reorder(EVENT_TYPE, n))

p1 <- ggplot(top_events, aes(x = n, y = EVENT_TYPE)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#27408B") +
  geom_text(aes(label = comma(n)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Top 15 Most Common Storm Event Types",
       x = "Number of Events", y = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma, expand = c(0.1, 0))

print(p1)
```

```{r}
# 2) TEMPORAL ANALYSIS
# Events over time
yearly_events <- StormEvents %>%
  group_by(YEAR) %>%
  summarise(
    total_events = n(),
    events_with_deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT > 0),
    events_with_injuries = sum(INJURIES_DIRECT > 0),
    total_deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT),
    total_injuries = sum(INJURIES_DIRECT)
  )

p2 <- ggplot(yearly_events, aes(x = YEAR, y = total_events)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2, color = "#27408B") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(title = "Storm Events Over Time (1958-Present)",
       x = "Year", y = "Number of Events") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma)

print(p2)

```

```{r}
# Monthly distribution
monthly_events <- StormEvents %>%
  mutate(MONTH_NUM = match(MONTH_NAME, month.name)) %>%
  group_by(MONTH_NUM, MONTH_NAME) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(MONTH_NAME = fct_reorder(MONTH_NAME, MONTH_NUM))

p3 <- ggplot(monthly_events, aes(x = MONTH_NAME, y = count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#CD6600") +
  labs(title = "Storm Events by Month (All Years)",
       x = "Month", y = "Number of Events") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma)

print(p3)

```

```{r}
# 3. GEOGRAPHIC ANALYSIS
# Top 20 states by event count
state_summary <- StormEvents %>%
  group_by(STATE) %>%
  summarise(
    total_events = n(),
    total_deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT + DEATHS_INDIRECT),
    total_injuries = sum(INJURIES_DIRECT + INJURIES_INDIRECT),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_events)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

p4 <- ggplot(state_summary, aes(x = reorder(STATE, total_events), y = total_events)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#27408B") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 20 States by Number of Storm Events",
       x = "", y = "Number of Events") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma)

print(p4)
```

```{r}
# Deadliest event types
deadly_events <- StormEvents %>%
  group_by(EVENT_TYPE) %>%
  summarise(
    total_deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT + DEATHS_INDIRECT),
    total_injuries = sum(INJURIES_DIRECT + INJURIES_INDIRECT),
    event_count = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(total_deaths > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_deaths)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

p5 <- ggplot(deadly_events, aes(x = reorder(EVENT_TYPE, total_deaths), y = total_deaths)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#CD6600") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Deadliest Storm Event Types",
       x = "", y = "Total Deaths") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p5)
```

```{r}
# 5. TORNADO-SPECIFIC ANALYSIS 
tornado_scale <- tornadoes %>%
    filter(!is.na(TOR_F_SCALE) & TOR_F_SCALE != "NA") %>%
    count(TOR_F_SCALE) %>%
    mutate(TOR_F_SCALE = factor(TOR_F_SCALE,
                                levels = c("EF0", "EF1", "EF2", "EF3", "EF4", "EF5",
                                         "F0", "F1", "F2", "F3", "F4", "F5")))
  
print(tornado_scale)

```

```{r}
p6 <- ggplot(tornado_scale, aes(x = TOR_F_SCALE, y = n)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#27408B") +
    labs(title = "Tornado Distribution by F/EF Scale",
         x = "F/EF Scale", y = "Number of Tornadoes") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  print(p6)
```

```{r}
# Tornado trends over time
  tornado_yearly <- tornadoes %>%
    group_by(YEAR) %>%
    summarise(
      count = n(),
      deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  p7 <- ggplot(tornado_yearly, aes(x = YEAR, y = count)) +
    geom_line(size = 1, color = "#CD6600") +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
    labs(title = "Tornado Events Over Time",
         x = "Year", y = "Number of Tornadoes") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  print(p7)

```

```{r}
# Geographic Mapping Analysis for Storm Events
library(tidyverse)
library(maps)
install.packages("mapdata")
library(mapdata)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
library(sf)
library(scales)
```

```{r}
# 4) Get US state map data
us_states <- map_data("state")
```

```{r}
# Prepare storm data by state
state_storm_summary <- StormEvents %>%
  mutate(region = tolower(STATE)) %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    total_events = n(),
    total_deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT + DEATHS_INDIRECT),
    total_injuries = sum(INJURIES_DIRECT + INJURIES_INDIRECT),
    tornado_count = sum(EVENT_TYPE == "Tornado"),
    events_per_year = n() / n_distinct(StormEvents$YEAR),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Merge with map data
map_data_storms <- us_states %>%
  left_join(state_storm_summary, by = "region")
```

```{r}
# Create choropleth map - Total Events
p8 <- ggplot(map_data_storms, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(fill = total_events), color = "white", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    name = "Total Events",
    low = "#27408B",      # Not severe
    high = "#CD6600",     # Severe
    midpoint = median(state_storm_summary$total_events, na.rm = TRUE),
    labels = comma
  ) +
  coord_map("albers", lat0 = 39, lat1 = 45) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Total Storm Events by State (1958-Present)",
       subtitle = "Orange indicates higher storm activity, Blue indicates lower activity") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

print(p8)
```
```{r}
# Calculate severity metrics INCLUDING pct_severe
state_storm_summary <- StormEvents %>%
  mutate(
    region = tolower(STATE),
    is_severe = (DEATHS_DIRECT > 0 | DEATHS_INDIRECT > 0 | 
                INJURIES_DIRECT > 0 | INJURIES_INDIRECT > 0 |
                EVENT_TYPE %in% c("Tornado", "Hurricane", "Tropical Storm"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    total_events = n(),
    severe_events = sum(is_severe),
    pct_severe = (severe_events / total_events) * 100,  # THIS IS THE MISSING COLUMN
    total_deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT + DEATHS_INDIRECT),
    total_injuries = sum(INJURIES_DIRECT + INJURIES_INDIRECT),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
# Merge with map data
map_data_storms <- us_states %>%
  left_join(state_storm_summary, by = "region")
```

```{r}
# Severity Categories with discrete colors
map_data_storms_cat <- map_data_storms %>%
  mutate(
    severity_category = case_when(
      total_events < quantile(state_storm_summary$total_events, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Low Activity",
      total_events < quantile(state_storm_summary$total_events, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Moderate Activity",
      total_events < quantile(state_storm_summary$total_events, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "High Activity",
      TRUE ~ "Severe Activity"
    ),
    severity_category = factor(severity_category, 
                              levels = c("Low Activity", "Moderate Activity", 
                                       "High Activity", "Severe Activity"))
  )

p9 <- ggplot(map_data_storms_cat, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(fill = severity_category), color = "white", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Storm Activity Level",
    values = c("Low Activity" = "#27408B",       # Blue
              "Moderate Activity" = "#4682B4",    # Lighter blue
              "High Activity" = "#CD853F",        # Light orange
              "Severe Activity" = "#CD6600")      # Dark orange
  ) +
  coord_map("albers", lat0 = 39, lat1 = 45) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Storm Activity Categories by State",
       subtitle = "Based on total storm event counts (quartiles)") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

print(p9)
```

```{r}
#Fatality-based severity map
p10 <- ggplot(map_data_storms, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(fill = total_deaths), color = "white", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    name = "Total\nDeaths",
    low = "#27408B",      # Low fatalities (blue)
    high = "#CD6600",     # High fatalities (orange)
    trans = "sqrt",       # Square root transformation for better visualization
    labels = comma
  ) +
  coord_map("albers", lat0 = 39, lat1 = 45) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Storm Fatalities by State",
       subtitle = "Cumulative storm-related deaths (Orange = More Deaths, Blue = Fewer Deaths)") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

print(p10)
```

```{r}
# Combined severity score
map_data_storms_score <- map_data_storms %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create a normalized severity score (0-100)
    events_norm = (total_events - min(state_storm_summary$total_events, na.rm = TRUE)) / 
                  (max(state_storm_summary$total_events, na.rm = TRUE) - 
                   min(state_storm_summary$total_events, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100,
    deaths_norm = (total_deaths - min(state_storm_summary$total_deaths, na.rm = TRUE)) / 
                  (max(state_storm_summary$total_deaths, na.rm = TRUE) - 
                   min(state_storm_summary$total_deaths, na.rm = TRUE)) * 100,
    severity_score = (events_norm * 0.5 + deaths_norm * 0.5)  # Weighted average
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

p11 <- ggplot(map_data_storms_score, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(fill = severity_score), color = "white", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    name = "Severity\nScore",
    low = "#27408B",      # Low severity (blue)
    high = "#CD6600",     # High severity (orange)
    labels = function(x) round(x, 0)
  ) +
  coord_map("albers", lat0 = 39, lat1 = 45) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Combined Storm Severity Score by State") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

print(p11)
```

```{r}
# 5) SUMMARY STATISTICS
# Overall statistics
overall_stats <- StormEvents %>%
  summarise(
    total_events = n(),
    events_with_deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT + DEATHS_INDIRECT > 0),
    events_with_injuries = sum(INJURIES_DIRECT + INJURIES_INDIRECT > 0),
    total_deaths = sum(DEATHS_DIRECT + DEATHS_INDIRECT),
    total_injuries = sum(INJURIES_DIRECT + INJURIES_INDIRECT),
    year_range = paste(min(YEAR), "-", max(YEAR))
  )

print(overall_stats)
```

```{r}
# Event type diversity
print(paste("Unique Event Types:", n_distinct(StormEvents$EVENT_TYPE)))
print(paste("Unique States:", n_distinct(StormEvents$STATE)))
print(paste("Events with Location Data:", nrow(storms_with_location), 
            "(", round(nrow(storms_with_location)/nrow(StormEvents)*100, 1), "%)"))
print(paste("Tornado Events:", nrow(tornadoes), 
            "(", round(nrow(tornadoes)/nrow(StormEvents)*100, 1), "%)"))
```

```{r}
# Deadliest single events
deadliest_single <- StormEvents %>%
  mutate(total_deaths = DEATHS_DIRECT + DEATHS_INDIRECT) %>%
  filter(total_deaths > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_deaths)) %>%
  select(EVENT_ID, STATE, EVENT_TYPE, BEGIN_DATE_TIME, total_deaths) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

print(deadliest_single)
```

```{r}
# Events with most injuries
most_injuries <- StormEvents %>%
  mutate(total_injuries = INJURIES_DIRECT + INJURIES_INDIRECT) %>%
  filter(total_injuries > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_injuries)) %>%
  select(EVENT_ID, STATE, EVENT_TYPE, BEGIN_DATE_TIME, total_injuries) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

print(most_injuries)
```

```{r}
# 6) DATA QUALITY INSIGHTS
# Check for data collection changes
data_changes <- StormEvents %>%
  group_by(YEAR) %>%
  summarise(
    events = n(),
    unique_types = n_distinct(EVENT_TYPE),
    pct_with_location = mean(!is.na(BEGIN_LAT)) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
# Find potential data collection change points
major_changes <- data_changes %>%
  mutate(
    event_change = events - lag(events),
    pct_change = (events - lag(events)) / lag(events) * 100
  ) %>%
  filter(abs(pct_change) > 50) %>%
  select(YEAR, events, pct_change)

```

```{r}
# Storm Summary statistics
severity_summary <- state_storm_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(total_events)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  mutate(
    severity_label = case_when(
      pct_severe > 5 ~ "High Severity",
      pct_severe > 2 ~ "Moderate Severity",
      TRUE ~ "Low Severity"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(region, total_events, severe_events, pct_severe, total_deaths, severity_label)
print(severity_summary)
```

```{r}
# Additional analysis: Which states have highest proportion of severe events?
high_severity_states <- state_storm_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_severe)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  select(region, total_events, pct_severe, total_deaths)

print(high_severity_states)
```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```
